course_description: Building upon PSYC8161 Neuroanatomy for Neuropsychologists and PSYC8171 Diagnosis and Management of Neuropsychological Disorders, this course pursues a theoretical perspective for the manifestation of neuropsychological disorders such as attention, memory, language and movement. The teaching material will focus on the findings of recent paradigms which combine neuroscience techniques (imaging, behavioural and physiological recordings) with cognitive psychology and neuropsychology. The aim of this course is to provide a sound understanding of the cognitive mechanisms underlying neuropsychological disorders.

course_contacts.notes: Our Student Advice Sessions are available to book via the following link: https://uqbookit.uq.edu.au/#/app/booking-types/c5971686-1e97-46b9-a595-ecf2221b7604 . The sessions are available via zoom, phone or in person. These sessions only run twice per week, on Tuesdays from 2pm-4pm and Thursdays from 9am-11am. There are eight 15 minute sessions in each 2 hour block, you just have to change the start and end times when you’re making the booking, as the system automatically defaults the booking to 1 hour in length. You are able to book into these sessions on the hour and then every 15 minutes from then onwards. For example Thursdays time slots will be 9:00am, 9:15am, 9:30am, 9:45am, 10:00am, 10:15am, 10:30am, 10:45am. There is no final timeslot at 11am. If you have any troubles booking one of the Student Advice Sessions, please give the School reception a call on 3365 6230.

course_aims: The aim of this course is to provide a sound understanding of the cognitive mechanisms underlying neuropsychological disorders and their treatment.

learning_outcomes.description: Gain an understanding of brain plasticity as applied to neuropsychology
Gain an understanding of emotional and psychological symptoms associated with certain pathologies in neuropsychology
Apply theoretical principles to the diagnosis and management of neuropsychological disorders
Have a solid understanding of theories of recovery and models of neuro-rehabilitation
Apply neuro-rehabilitation to treat a range of neuropsychological disorders and evaluate outcomes

assessment_details.title: Case Presentation and Reflection
assessment_details.due_date: 22/04/2026 5:00 pm 29/04/2026 5:00 pm The due date for the Presentation (Part 1, 40% Weighting) is 22/4/2026 The due date for the Reflection (Part 2, 10% Weighting) is one week later: 29/4/2026, by 5pm
assessment_details.task_description: This assessment has two integrated components designed to evaluate your ability to (a) apply neuropsychological theory and assessment to clinical formulation and rehabilitation planning, and (b) critically reflect on rehabilitation processes and techniques used in practice. Part 1: Case Presentation (20 minutes, 10 mins Q&A; 40% weighting): Students will present an individual clinical case appropriate for neuropsychological rehabilitation drawn from their own case load. The presentation should demonstrate: an integrated understanding of the client's neuropsychological profile (diagnosis/presenting condition) a single cognitive domain for rehabilitation (e.g., attention, executive function, memory social cognition) with rationale for choice and mechanisms underlying impairment (e.g., working memory load, inhibitory control) and linking to real-world functional difficulties, and rehabilitation goals (specific to the cognitive domain) and plan (the proposed approach e.g., restorative, compensatory), specific technique(s) used (e.g., errorless learning, goal management training), the influence of wider factors (e.g., insight, motivation, learning capacity), and evaluation strategy to monitor outcomes. With only 20 mins, you need to plan your time to ensure you cover all components Assessment criteria: 0-5: Neuropsychological profile lacks detail; poor understanding of underlying cognitive mechanisms underlying presentation; inappropriate cognitive domain for treatment lacking rationale for choice; lacking appropriate goals, plan, and evaluation strategy; and minimal response to questions 6-10: Basic (undeveloped) neuropsychological profile and understanding of cognitive mechanisms; limited rationale for cognitive treatment, and underspecified goals, domain application to formulation and treatment of neuropsychological disorders; and limited response to questions. 11-15: A clear and coherent neuropsychological profile supported by a good understanding of cognitive mechanisms, with few gaps in knowledge; a clear evidence-informed rationale for treatment domain and approach; detailed goals, plan and evaluation strategy; and considered response to questions. 16-20: Excellent and thorough understanding of the client's presentation and cognitive mechanisms underlying presentation; insightful and well-argued rationale for treatment domain and approach; clearly specified goals, plan and evaluation strategy; and responses se to questions shows an excellent understanding of the client's case. Part 2: Reflection (500 words; 10% weighting): This assignment requires students to write a short reflection on one rehabilitation technique, process, or approach used in their case presentation, that draws both on the student’s own reflections on the case and those arising from questions and commentary following the presentation. This is not a description task; the focus is on critical reflection and learning . Your reflection piece should include: a brief description of the chosen technique/process and its role in the case, why it is appropriate for your client, with consideration of theory or evidence to support its application and reflection on the potential strengths and limitations of the approach Assessment criteria 0-5: Poor reflection with limited (if any) learning or self-awareness; reflection lacks integration with theory/evidence; failure to consider strengths and weaknesses of approach used; poorly written and structured narrative and/or outside of word count. 6-10: Basic, more descriptive, reflection demonstrating an underdeveloped sense of learning and self-awareness; limited integration of theory/evidence into reflection; undeveloped consideration of strengths and weaknesses of approach; and poorly structured narrative and/or outside of word limit. 11-15: Clear and coherent reflection with a good understanding of the learning and self-awareness gained; Solid integration of theory/evidence into reflection; a good understanding of the strengths and limitations of the approach used; well written and structured with only minor issues and within the word count 16-20: Excellent and insightful critical reflection demonstrating learning and self-awareness; clear integration of theory/evidence into reflection; fully integrated analysis of strengths and limitations of approach and its appropriateness to the case; clearly written and structured reflection within the word count. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The reflection must be submitted via turnitin, one week after the presentation; 29/4/2026 by 5pm
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
For full details please review the School of Psychology Student Guidelines - Extensions and Deferred quiz/exam (the web link can be found on the Policies and Procedures tab)
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Rehabilitation Case Study (3,000 words)
assessment_details.task_description: To demonstrate the capacity to undertake a co-ordinated analysis and formulation for rehabilitation of a case with neuropsychological disorder. Students are given a case description and outline neuropsychological profile and are assessed on their capacity to (i) interpret the profile (ii) provide a formulation for rehabilitation (iii) specify and justify a program of rehabilitation, and (iv) identify objective indicators against which rehabilitation progress and treatment outcome will be assessed. Assessment Criteria: 0- 5: Poor understanding of cognitive mechanisms underlying presentation; poor application to formulation and treatment of neuropsychological disorders. 6-10: Basic (undeveloped) understanding of cognitive mechanisms underlying presentation; limited application to formulation and treatment of neuropsychological disorders. 11-15: Moderate understanding of cognitive mechanisms underlying presentation, but some gaps in knowledge; reasonable application to formulation and treatment of neuropsychological disorders 16-20: Excellent and thorough understanding of cognitive mechanisms underlying presentation, with few (if any) evident gaps in knowledge; insightful and well-argued application to formulation and treatment of neuropsychological disorders. Read full task description Hide full task description

learning_activities.topic: Models of Recovery, Assessment, Presentation and Treatment Three topic areas will be covered in today's teaching: Models of Recovery in Neurorehabilitation, Assessment and Case Formulation, Neuropsychiatric Conditions (Dr Emmaline Falconer) Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
Presentation and Treatment Two topics will be covered: Functional Neurological Disorders (Dr Megan Broughton) Treatment of Executive Disorders Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
Presentation and Treatment Two sessions will cover: Treatment of Executive Disorders, Treatment of Memory Disorders Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
Presentation and Treatment Two topics to cover today: Treatment of Memory Disorders, Dementia Rehabilitation Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
Case Presentation Assignment Today's session will focus on the case presentation assignment with students presenting their own individual cases. Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05

Applying Rehabilitation Principles We'll cover three topics in today's session: Identity Change in Rehabilitation, Psychosocial Intervention, Driver Assessment and Management Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L05
Applying Rehabilitation Principles The main focus of today's session will be on Technology and Telehealth in Rehabilitation Learning outcomes: L03, L05
